purpose: Cross-validation of this repo's TRADESTAT-derived FY2025-26 merchandise trade figures against three additional official sources requested for verification: the Commerce Ministry's Trade Intelligence and Analytics (TIA) Portal, DGFT's Trade Statistics & Analytics hub, PIB press releases, and DPIIT's 2025-26 Annual Report.

tia_portal.source_note: Department of Commerce dashboard, data sourced from DGCIS -- same lineage as TRADESTAT
tia_portal.discrepancy_note: Imports and the HS27 export figure match this repo's TRADESTAT pull almost to the dollar. The TIA Portal's own total export figure (451.07bn) reads about 2% / $9.3bn higher than both this repo's TRADESTAT figure and PIB's official release (see below) -- likely a data-vintage/revision difference given FY2025-26 is still provisional on all sources, not a methodology error, since two of three independent sources agree closely.

dgft.finding: DGFT's 'Import Export Data Bank' link resolves directly to tradestat.commerce.gov.in -- the same source this repo's trade-balance bulletin already scrapes, not an independent dataset. DGFT's 'Latest Trade Figures' link routes to commerce.gov.in, which is PIB-sourced (see below).

pib.source: PIB press release, Ministry of Commerce and Industry, FY2025-26 (April-March) merchandise trade release
pib.note: Exports and deficit match this repo's TRADESTAT figures within a rounding error ($441.78bn vs $441.7bn; -$333.19bn vs -$334.3bn).

dpiit_annual_report_2025_26.does_not_publish: A merchandise trade-balance or trade-deficit series -- consistent with what this repo already found when checking DPIIT for services-trade data (see services_trade_and_overall_balance json).

iem_part_a_investment_intention_rs_crore.note: Appendix IV, 'Sectorwise Investment Intention based on Part A during last five years' -- Industrial Entrepreneur Memorandum filings, a completely separate data system (industrial licensing, not customs). Used here only as independent corroboration of which sectors are seeing the biggest domestic capacity build-out, not as a trade-data source.

dpiit_annual_report_2025_26.champion_subsectors_note: DPIIT's 24 champion sub-sectors programme explicitly lists 'electronics' and 'aluminium' among sectors chosen partly for 'need for import substitution' -- language matching this repo's independent finding (via trade data alone) that electrical machinery and related chapters are the fastest-growing high-value imports.

fdi_context.note: Top-line FDI context only -- DPIIT's report does not break FDI equity inflows down into a text-extractable sector table in this edition (presented as a chart image); not used for chapter-level corroboration.

dpiit_annual_report_2025_26.hs_code_confirmation: The report's own industry-specific sections (watches HS91, toys HS95, glass HS70) cite HS-code export/import tables sourced from the 'Department of Commerce Trade portal' -- confirming DPIIT itself relies on the same DGCIS/TRADESTAT lineage as this repo's bulletins.
